request.description: Create a new user account with BetterAuth integration. Protected route (requires valid auth). Flow: (1) Creates BetterAuth user, (2) Creates/links Monade user via ba_user_id foreign key. Returns user details and BetterAuth session token.

Onboarding linking step for dashboard flow. Requires BetterAuth session cookie from sign-up/sign-in (send request with credentials/cookies). Links current authenticated BetterAuth user to Monade users table by ba_user_id, or creates the user if missing.

request.description: Update the speaking accent for a specific assistant.
Retrieve the tools configuration for a specific assistant. Returns enableTools master toggle and toolsConfig JSON with per-tool settings.
Update the tools configuration for a specific assistant. Supports vertex_rag tool type. Set toolsConfig to null to remove all tools. Each tool has its own enabled toggle.
Master toggle to enable or disable all tools for a specific assistant. When enableTools is false, no tools are sent to the agent regardless of individual tool settings.

request.description: Create a new RAG corpus in Vertex AI and upload a file. The file is stored in GCS and imported into the corpus. Returns the corpus with its corpusResourceName which can be used in assistant toolsConfig. Provide either file_text (plain text) or file_base64 (base64 encoded file).

  "call_id": "call_123456789",

    "summary": "Call was successful and resulted in positive outcome",

      "Customer expressed interest in product",

    ],
    "call_quality": "HIGH",
    "use_case": "SALES"
  },
  "transcript_url": "https://storage.googleapis.com/bucket/transcript.jsonl",
  "phone_number": "+1234567890",
  "campaign_id": "campaign_123",
  "sip_call_id": "DlMhXreVnNfErYO5BpFcPcbLsb1"
}

request.description: Submit new analytics data for a call. This endpoint is typically called by the post-processing pipeline after analyzing a transcript. Requires call_id, user_uid, analytics object, transcript_url, and phone_number. Optional sip_call_id (Vobiz call ID from sip.callIDFull) enables direct recording lookup.

item.name: Get User Analytics by User ID

header.description: API Key or Service Token for authentication

request.description: Retrieve all analytics records for a specific user by their user_uid.

header.description: API Key for authentication

request.description: Retrieve analytics data for a specific user's campaign. Requires both user_uid and campaign_id.

header.description: API Key or Service Token for authentication

request.description: Get analytical statistics for a specific user, including total calls, verdict distribution, average confidence score, and call quality distribution.

header.description: API Key or Service Token for authentication

request.description: Fetch the call recording URL from Vobiz for a specific call. Uses sip_call_id (Vobiz call ID from sip.callIDFull) for direct 1:1 recording lookup via GET /Recording/{sip_call_id}/. On first request, fetches from Vobiz API and caches the recording_url in the database. Subsequent requests return the cached URL instantly. Returns recording_url (direct WAV/MP3 link for audio playback), recording_id, and duration_ms. Requires sip_call_id to be stored in the analytics record (propagated from LiveKit participant attributes). If the recording is not yet available (Vobiz processing delay), returns 404 with a 'try again' message.

header.description: API Key or Service Token for authentication

request.description:
Kick off async recording preparation pipeline. The backend fetches the recording from Vobiz (using sip_call_id), downloads the audio file, uploads it to GCS temp storage, and generates a 30-minute signed URL for streaming/download.

Returns immediately with status. Frontend should poll the /status endpoint every 2-3 seconds.

**Response statuses:**
- `{"status": "processing"}` — Pipeline started, poll /status for updates
- `{"status": "ready", "url": "...", "download_url": "..."}` — Cache hit (repeat click within 30min), url is for streaming (<audio src>), download_url triggers browser download
- `{"status": "failed", "error": "..."}` — Previous attempt failed

**Error responses:**
- 404: Analytics record not found or no sip_call_id available
- 503: Cloud storage or Vobiz not configured

**Notes:**
- Preparation takes 15-40 seconds depending on file size
- Signed URLs expire after 30 minutes; repeat requests within that window return cached URLs instantly
- Both .mp3 and .wav formats are supported
- The url field returns an inline signed URL (for <audio> playback with seeking support)
- The download_url field returns an attachment signed URL (triggers browser save-as dialog)

item.name: BetterAuth (Authentication)
item.description: BetterAuth-powered authentication endpoints. Handles signup, login, session management, and API key management. All /api/auth/* routes are public (no pre-existing auth required). Architecture note: `lib/auth.js` initializes BetterAuth and mounts these `/api/auth/*` handlers; `routes/_auth.js` is separate middleware that protects non-public `/api/*` routes by validating session/API key/service-token and enforcing user ownership. `_auth.js` does not create users; app access requires an existing BetterAuth -> Monade link (created via backend onboarding route `POST /api/users`).

request.description: Register a BetterAuth identity only (session/auth-layer). For backend-driven onboarding, use `POST /api/users` to create/link the Monade user record required by protected app APIs.
Authenticate with email and password. Returns a session cookie (Set-Cookie header) and user object. The cookie is used automatically for subsequent dashboard requests.

header.description: BetterAuth API Key (or omit and use session cookie)

request.description: Get the current session. Works with either a session cookie OR a BetterAuth API key (Authorization: Bearer <key>). Returns session and user object if authenticated, null otherwise. Use this after sign-in/sign-up to verify auth state and extract BetterAuth user identity.
Sign out and invalidate the current session cookie.
Create a new BetterAuth API key for the authenticated user. Requires an active session (cookie). The returned key can be used as 'Authorization: Bearer <key>' for SDK/API access.
List all API keys for the authenticated user. Requires an active session (cookie).
Revoke/delete a BetterAuth API key by its ID. Requires an active session (cookie).

header.description: BetterAuth API Key (or omit and use session cookie)

request.description: Returns the authenticated user's Monade profile (user_uid, email, name, credits). Works with either session cookie or BetterAuth API key. This is the primary endpoint for app/business APIs after login because it resolves BetterAuth user -> Monade user_uid used by protected routes and ownership checks.
Health check endpoint to verify the service is running and accessible.

header.description: API Key or Service Token for authentication

request.description: Retrieve a list of all SIP trunks registered with LiveKit. Returns both outbound (trunk_type='outbound') and inbound (trunk_type='inbound') trunks.
Create a new SIP trunk in LiveKit. The trunk will be registered globally and can be used by any user. Outbound trunks (default) make calls; inbound trunks receive calls.

header.description: API Key or Service Token for authentication

request.description: Retrieve details of a specific SIP trunk by its ID.

Partial update of a global SIP trunk in LiveKit. Only fields included in the body are changed — omitted fields (including auth_password) are left untouched. Trunk ID is preserved (no delete+recreate).

header.description: API Key or Service Token for authentication

request.description: Delete a SIP trunk from LiveKit. This removes it globally and makes it unavailable to all users.

header.description: API Key or Service Token for authentication

request.description: Retrieve a SIP trunk from LiveKit by its name.

Initiate an outbound SIP call using a user-specific trunk. Requires user_uid for trunk ownership validation. The selected trunk will be synced to LiveKit if needed, then an outbound call is placed. Agent dispatch is created with prompt metadata baked in.
Create a new SIP inbound trunk in LiveKit for receiving incoming calls.
List all SIP inbound trunks from LiveKit.
Get a specific inbound trunk by its LiveKit trunk ID.
Update an existing inbound trunk (partial update — only send fields to change).
Delete an inbound trunk from LiveKit.
Create a dispatch rule with pre-baked prompt. Fetches the assistant's knowledge base, builds the full prompt, and stores it in LiveKit so inbound calls get instant agent response.
List all SIP dispatch rules from LiveKit.
Get a specific dispatch rule by its ID.
Re-bake a dispatch rule with fresh prompt data. Call this when an assistant's knowledge base is updated.
Delete a dispatch rule from LiveKit.
Health check endpoint. Returns Redis status, configuration, and count of rooms that have been signaled for disconnect.

variable.description: The user ID to list active sessions for

request.description: List all active call sessions for a user. Returns each session's phone_number, room_name, call_id, duration_seconds, and started_at timestamp. Use this to populate a dashboard showing live calls.

query.description: Reason for disconnecting (e.g., manual_disconnect, user_request, admin_action)

variable.description: The LiveKit room name to disconnect

request.description: Send a graceful disconnect signal to a specific LiveKit room. The agent says a natural farewell and disconnects within ~30 seconds. All shutdown callbacks (transcript save, billing end, campaign notification) fire automatically.

query.description: Optional user ID filter. If provided, only disconnects if the call belongs to this user.

variable.description: The phone number of the active call to disconnect (e.g., +919876543210)

request.description: Disconnect an active call by phone number. Looks up the room from Redis and sends a graceful disconnect signal. Since one phone number can only have one active call, this is a safe 1:1 lookup. Returns the matched room_name and call_id. Agent says farewell and disconnects within ~30 seconds.
